Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200      .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", Embedded admin UI — assets from `ui/build` compiled into the binary.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The `UiAssets` struct embeds every file under `ui/build` at compile time.\n4\t//! The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n5\t//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n6\t//! Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

Manage beads — portable, ephemeral work items with metadata.

Beads provide a lightweight work queue for AI agents and developers.
They track tasks, dependencies, and status without coupling to any
specific workflow methodology.

Examples:
  ddx bead init                                    # Initialize bead storage
  ddx bead create "Fix auth bug" --type bug        # Create a bead
  ddx bead list --status open                      # List open beads
  ddx bead ready                                   # Show beads ready for work
  ddx bead dep add <id> <dep-id>                   # Add a dependency
  ddx bead import --from jsonl beads.jsonl          # Import from JSONL

Usage:
  ddx bead [flags]
  ddx bead [command]

Aliases:
  bead, beads

Available Commands:
  ac-check              Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree
  archive               Archive closed beads when active beads.jsonl exceeds size threshold
  blocked               List externally blocked beads
  clear-cooldown        Bulk-clear work cooldowns across the tracker
  close                 Close a bead
  cooldown              Inspect and clear work cooldowns
  create                Create a new bead
  dep                   Manage bead dependencies
  doctor                Detect (and optionally repair) beads.jsonl rows with oversized fields or back-edge deps
  evidence              Manage append-only execution evidence
  export                Export beads as JSONL
  human                 Deprecated alias: resolve proposed operator-attention beads
  import                Import beads from external source
  init                  Initialize bead storage
  lint                  Check acceptance criteria mechanical verifiability
  list                  List beads
  merge                 Resolve a Git conflict in .ddx/beads.jsonl
  metrics               Per-bead cost, token, and attempt summaries from execution evidence
  migrate               Split active beads.jsonl into beads + beads-archive + attachments
  needs-human           Deprecated alias: list proposed operator-attention beads
  operator-attention    List wedge/timeout lease releases needing operator attention
  queue                 Manage in-priority queue ordering
  ready                 List beads ready for work
  reap                  List (or close) epics whose children are all terminal
  reconcile             Reconcile stale bead lifecycle metadata
  reconcile-attachments Commit dirty attachment audit files
  reopen                Reopen a closed bead
  replay                Re-execute a prior attempt with alternate model/harness/prompt
  replay-bench          Run multiple replays in parallel against the same source attempt
  review                Generate a review prompt for a bead's implementation
  routing               Show routing decisions recorded for a bead
  show                  Show a bead
  status                Show bead counts and active work
  update                Update a bead
  validate-ready        Validate execution-ready beads have machine-measurable acceptance

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for bead

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
      --no-update-check            Disable the automatic update check (network call) on each command
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx bead [command] --help" for more information about a command.

data.text: The generic DDx docs already expose a `no_evidence_produced` path, which makes the empty bead look like an intentional no-op rather than a missing implementation. I’m doing one focused search for the bead title and any nearby provenance docs before I decide whether to write a rationale or stop as already satisfied.

input.command: /usr/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "Manual provenance|manual provenance|provenance" docs .ddx cli internal library -S'

data.output:
# Execute-Bead Self-Commit Contract

`ddx try` runs an agent inside an isolated worktree. The agent must produce
exactly one of two durable signals before it exits:

- a git commit containing the completed implementation and tests
- `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt`

The rationale file is only for attempts that cannot produce a valid commit. It
must explain what is done, what is blocking, and what a follow-up attempt needs.
DDx treats that as a legitimate `no_changes` signal.

If the agent exits without a commit and without the rationale file, DDx records
`no_evidence_produced`. That status is a harness failure, not success and not
ordinary no-changes. DDx does not land an execution-evidence-only iteration, and
the reviewer does not need to spend a pass discovering that the diff contains
only `.ddx/executions` metadata.

When the worktree still has uncommitted paths at that point, DDx includes those
paths in `result.json` as `no_evidence_paths` so an operator can diagnose
whether the agent edited ignored files, failed to commit, or wrote outside the
intended scope.

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:21:{"id":"corpus-148d699c1390","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. But the contract never defines what ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, and ddx.cost.pricing_ref should look like when all child chat costs are known yet their provenance differs across turns, such as one upstream-service-reported turn plus one configured-cost turn. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only route-targets single-attempt provenance. The current implementation fell into this gap and downgraded a known total to unknown when provenance metadata differed.
Suggested fix: Amend the root-span cost-rollup section to specify a conformant representation for mixed known-cost provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. Add a contract-focused regression test or checklist entry covering mixed upstream-service-reported/configured sessions.","acceptance":"CONTRACT-001 explicitly defines how the root invoke_agent span represents known session totals when child chat spans have mixed known provenance; the contract no longer requires an impossible or ambiguous ddx.cost.source/pricing_ref combination at the root; conformance tests or checklist coverage include a mixed upstream-service-reported/configured session example.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-7853958b","title":"correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:33:{"id":"corpus-2205629c24fa","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:289
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a upstream-service-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.
Suggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.","acceptance":"A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed upstream-service-reported/configured cost rollups.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-264e7ea0","title":"correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. FEAT-004 requires unknown fields to be preserved on read/write operations, and FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as durable replay/audit provenance. Losing that field on an unrelated closed bead silently corrupts the replay baseline for the SD-018 verification task even though this commit did not govern that bead. Suggested fix: repair hx-ddx-agent-target-verify so its closing_commit_sha is restored to 2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and add/adjust tracker update coverage so finalizing one bead cannot clobber preserved fields on unrelated rows.","acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-ddx-agent-target-verify --json reports closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and regression coverage proves updating or closing hx-9c1a1558 leaves unrelated bead fields unchanged","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:agent","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"hx-a51dd27a","title":"correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:126:{"id":"corpus-862310dae8d9","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:518
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. In a clean temp git repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' dirty with ' M .ddx/beads.jsonl'. The landed HEAD commit 9494afc82408cac9a650d616fc3207197e1dfbd6 contains only the status=open->closed change, while the durable closing_commit_sha appears only in the uncommitted worktree diff. That means the command still fails to land close provenance atomically and leaves replay/audit metadata outside the recorded close commit.
Suggested fix: Restructure bead close so the recorded closing_commit_sha is present in the same landed commit that closes the bead, or perform and persist a second deterministic commit/update so git status is clean after close and HEAD contains the final provenance-bearing row.","acceptance":"With git.auto_commit=always in a git repo and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' clean, and 'git show HEAD -- .ddx/beads.jsonl' includes the bead row with closing_commit_sha equal to 'git rev-parse HEAD' rather than requiring an extra uncommitted worktree update","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"bead":{"id":"hx-73c95bab","title":"integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:130:{"id":"corpus-8da9c3bacad5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:80
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. That is too broad: .ddx contains real governed artifacts such as config and other tracked workspace files, not just tracker backfills. In a temp repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, creating a bead, modifying a tracked .ddx/notes.txt file, and running ddx bead close lands a close commit that changes .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/notes.txt, but the bead still omits closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.
Suggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.","acceptance":"With git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, closing a bead whose landed implementation changes only tracked .ddx/* files still records closing_commit_sha for the landed close commit, and ddx agent replay can use that commit boundary instead of falling back to sessionless/manual mode.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"bead":{"id":"hx-98fec407","title":"correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:131:{"id":"corpus-8e69723cf1b4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:649
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.
Suggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","acceptance":"A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"hx-939516c7","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.
Suggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","acceptance":"A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"]}}
library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:134:{"id":"corpus-906830fa2998","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.
Suggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance route-target that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.","acceptance":"ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"bead":{"id":"hx-63ac1232","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.

But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.
Suggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance route-target that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.","acceptance":"ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"]}}

Key upstream changes since `v0.1.0`:
- OTel telemetry landed for session, chat-attempt, and tool spans/metrics, with a documented telemetry contract and runtime-specific cost attribution.
- Upstream-service retries moved into the runtime so upstream-services execute one attempt per call and expose richer attempt metadata.
- Session continuity/history support landed in `Run`.
- `ddx-agent usage` reporting landed.
- Tool path handling now resolves symlink chains.
- Compaction behavior was hardened: prefix-aware accounting, balanced compaction events, and fail-closed handling when compaction cannot fit within budget.
- Self-update cache writing was fixed for first-run homes without an existing cache directory.
- Mixed-provenance known-cost session rollups now preserve the root known cost instead of downgrading to unknown.

Align the governing docs around one execute-bead evidence contract.

- Define the execute-bead prompt template as a DDx-owned rationalizer over bead fields plus resolved governing references, not a bead mutation and not a speculative summary of whole specs
- Define the stable tracked artifact set for each execution attempt: prompt, manifest, result, checks, normalized log, and usage/upstream-service pointers
- Define the split between tracked execution evidence and ignored local scratch/runtime state
- Define the default commit policy for successful and preserved attempts

Align the governing docs around one execute-bead evidence contract.

## Goals
- Define the execute-bead prompt template as a DDx-owned rationalizer over bead fields plus resolved governing references, not a bead mutation and not a speculative summary of whole specs

Scope of PRD update:

(1) Restructure Problem section into 6 clusters keyed to thesis principles in product-vision.md:
- Abstraction (no structure / no composition / no integrity guarantees / no transferability)
- Iteration over tracked work (no reusable work-item store / no reusable execution evidence)
- Methodology plurality (no reusable agent dispatch — every workflow tool reinvents)
- LLM physics (NEW cluster — token cost as a permanent constraint; nothing in current PRD)
- Evidence/provenance (no provenance for generated artifacts; no measurement; no feedback capture)
- Human-AI collaboration (no composition for handoffs; no discoverability; no network access)

Existing problem bullets re-grouped under these headers; add the LLM-physics cluster.

(2) Goals (primary): reword #1; add three-layer-architecture goal; add generated-artifact-regeneration goal; add 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP goal.

(3) Non-goals: sharpen loop wording — DDx owns mechanical queue drain (ddx work); content-aware supervisory decisions remain plugin/HELIX. Add: 'DDx does not catalog run types beyond the three layers.'

(4) Feature blurbs: FEAT-001 (CLI: run/try/work top-level + ddx agent passthrough); FEAT-005 (multi-media identity); FEAT-006 (layer-1 consumer + structural passthrough); FEAT-007 (sidecar + generated_by + separate staleness); FEAT-008/021 (media-type rendering + regenerate + layer-aware run views); FEAT-010 (3-layer architecture + substrate unification); FEAT-019 (child of FEAT-010 — evaluation UX, workflow shapes -> skills).

Summary: artifacts (multi-media); three-layer run architecture; one substrate; invocation upstream.

Add `TestExecuteBeadLandingCommitsFinalResultArtifact` (or equivalent named regression) proving a successful land rewrites `result.json` to final orchestrator fields and leaves no unstaged or staged diff for `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/result.json` in the project root.
2. The same test must assert the final committed `result.json` contains `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `outcome: "merged"`, and `orchestrator_status: "success"`.
3. Add or extend an existing evidence/cleanup test proving the final-result commit does not stage embedded session logs or `.ddx/run-state/**`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecuteBeadLandingCommitsFinalResultArtifact|Test.*FinalResult.*RunState|Test.*FinalResult.*Embedded" -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","closing_commit_sha":"2066886f9ce5b6182e5e0af43df90ce2614d5bdb","created_at":"2026-05-15T16:22:54.854682964Z","description":"PROBLEM
Successful `ddx work` landings still leave `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/result.json` modified in the operator checkout after the worker exits or self-replaces. This happened twice during the current monitor run: `ddx-8a0aea8e` left `.ddx/executions/20260515T154605-7c45a217/result.json` dirty with `outcome` changing from `task_succeeded` to `merged`, and `ddx-7e422ff6` left `.ddx/executions/20260515T161644-6d9781bd/result.json` dirty the same way. In both cases the operator had to create an extra `chore: finalize execution result ...` commit manually. That violates the no-dirty-root invariant for normal worker success.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1582-1588` documents that the worker writes a preliminary `result.json` before committing so the implementation commit message can be sourced from tracked artifact data. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:850-868` commits the evidence bundle during landing, but that commit happens before the orchestrator result rewrite. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2605-2626` then rewrites `result.json` in the project root with final fields such as `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `outcome=merged`, and `orchestrator_status`. No follow-up staging/commit path captures that final rewrite, so the tracked `result.json` remains dirty after otherwise successful landings.

PROPOSED FIX
After `WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact` rewrites a tracked execution `result.json` in the project root, stage and commit just that final result artifact (or fold it into the evidence commit by moving the final rewrite before evidence commit) using the same git/index-lock recovery path as other DDx-managed audit commits. The final branch history must contain the final orchestrator result fields and `git status --short` must be clean for that attempt.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` only if final-result timing belongs in landing
- Focused execute-bead landing tests under `cli/internal/agent/`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not track embedded session logs or `.ddx/run-state/**`.
- Do not change the result JSON schema.
- Do not remove the preliminary result write used for commit-message provenance unless the replacement still preserves that contract.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` or existing evidence files.","events_attachment":"ddx-117158a2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-117158a2","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","area:git","dirty-tree","kind:bug","operator-ux"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":630,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-7d6f91f5","status":"closed","title":"work: auto-commit final execution result rewrite","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:29.919512121Z"}

Evolve execute-bead from a transient worktree runner into a tracked execution-evidence workflow.

## Scope
- Define an explicit execute-bead prompt template / rationalizer contract
- Define tracked execution artifacts for each attempt, separate from ignored local runtime scratch
- Define which execution evidence is committed on landed and preserved attempts
- Define how commit messages are rendered from tracked execution artifact files
- Keep provider-native transcripts external by default while tracking normalized pointers, usage, and summaries in DDx-owned artifacts","events_attachment":"ddx-32b3008e/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-32b3008e","issue_type":"epic","labels":["helix","kind:architecture","area:agent","area:exec","area:git","area:docs"],"notes":"Architecture/planning epic. The fixtures must be self-contained and must not reference Niflheim paths as required inputs.
2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.
4. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
5. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentIsExecutionIneligible` simulates `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`; it asserts the parent is marked execution-ineligible/proposed or dependency-waiting according to policy and that no further implementation attempt or min-power escalation is scheduled.
6. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile. Land coordination, repeated pre-claim system warnings, decomposed-parent outcomes, and dirty generated DDx evidence must not raise min power.
7. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, review status/verdict or explicit skip reason, and land/reconcile status for the Niflheim scenario families.
8. Existing related behavior from `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, and `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered; this bead either reuses their helpers or waits for those dependencies, and does not add duplicate low-level implementations.
9. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|EmptyReviewResult|NilReviewer|LandCoordination|DecomposedParent|RetryEscalation|CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
10. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-02T22:52:12Z","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:41.332809555Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-334c0958","depends_on_id":"ddx-2965925e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:53Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
A live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle "Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.

This bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.

BACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE
The evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.

1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title "Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing".
- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.
- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.
- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.
- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:
  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`
  `powerClass=standard`
  `rationale: status: open`
  `orchestrator_action: decompose`
  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`
  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`
  `outcome_reason=decomposed`
- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.

2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title "Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8".
- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.
- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.
- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.
  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.
  - Error detail:
    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`
  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision="success"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.
- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:
  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.
- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.

3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.
- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title "Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata".
- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:
  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`
- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.
- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.

4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.
- Recent Niflheim "success" attempts had empty review results:
  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.
  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision="success"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.
  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision="success"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.
- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.
- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.
- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.
- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.
- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.
- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.
- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.
- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.
- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.
- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.
- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.
- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","events_attachment":"ddx-334c0958/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["adr:024","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:review","area:workers","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","prevention","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021"],"notes":"decomposed into ddx-044cd257, ddx-3a6f1ad6, ddx-d4817c9b, ddx-01aa7b18, ddx-1865eadc","parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"queue-rank":1850,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-17T20:08:03.826920996Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

Evolve the synthesized execute-bead prompt from markdown-heading sections to an explicit XML-tagged structure.

## Goals
- Replace markdown section headings in the synthesized execute-bead prompt with XML-style tags for machine-significant structure
- Preserve the current human-readable content while making the prompt easier to parse, diff, and validate deterministically
- Define which sections are required, optional, and repeatable in the prompt template
- Keep the prompt aligned with the tracked execution manifest and future commit-provenance rendering
- Update the governing specs and contracts so the prompt-template contract is documented rather than only implied by code

## Required spec work
- Update FEAT-006 and any applicable execute-bead contract docs to define the prompt-template structure
- Align the execution-evidence lane docs so prompt.md is explicitly structured, machine-readable evidence

## Required implementation work
- Update prompt synthesis in cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go to emit XML-tagged sections
- Update automated coverage to assert the new tagged structure and prevent markdown-header regression","events_attachment":"ddx-4a91da5d/events.jsonl","execution-eligible":true,"id":"ddx-4a91da5d","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:planning","kind:architecture","area:agent","area:exec","area:docs"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-802b0d0","spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Adopt XML-tagged execute-bead prompt template","updated_at":"2026-04-14T04:24:59.363312762Z","work-last-detail":"agent execution failed","work-last-status":"execution_failed","work-retry-after":"2026-04-14T02:45:20Z"}

Backend:
- GraphQL mutation artifactRegenerate(artifactId: ID!): ArtifactRegenerateResult
- ArtifactRegenerateResult: { runId: ID!, status: String! }
- Resolver dispatches a server-side worker using the artifact's generator/prompt, returns the new run id.
- If no generator metadata, mutation returns a typed error (not a crash).

UI:
- Regenerate button in provenance panel (only shown when generated_by is present).
- On click: button shows spinner; calls mutation; on success shows returned run id and two links: 'View run' (/runs/:runId) and 'Refresh artifact' (reload artifact detail).
- On error: show error message inline in provenance panel; no silent failure.

Integration test: crashed worker leaves stale run-state that orphan-recovery cleans up","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","closing_commit_sha":"3d42eb69b6dbca8fab51eeb76a4bb37e6e8c1e63","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:54:01.303740193Z","description":"During ddx agent execute-bead and ddx agent work, write a .ddx/run-state.json file at execution start and remove it on completion. This file records the currently-executing bead ID, attempt ID, harness, start time, and execution worktree path so operators and HELIX can observe what is currently running without polling the bead tracker.

This capability is implied by CONTRACT-001 Section 5 (Always-on runtime metrics and provenance) but is currently absent from the DDx source. The post-execution evidence bundle (.ddx/executions/) captures historical facts, but there is no live-state signal during execution.

## Scope

- On execute-bead start: write .ddx/run-state.json with fields: bead_id, attempt_id, harness, model, started_at (RFC3339), worktree_path
- On execute-bead end (success or failure): remove .ddx/run-state.json (or overwrite with status=idle)
- work may write N run-state entries or a single active one depending on parallelism model
- File must be written atomically (write tmp + rename) to avoid partial-read races

## Contract reference

CONTRACT-001 Section 5: Always-on runtime metrics and provenance — per-attempt capture of harness, model, session ID, elapsed, tokens, cost, base revision, result revision.","events_attachment":"ddx-a730058a/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-a730058a","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:agent","kind:feat","phase:build","contract:CONTRACT-001"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 91f17a05b054b92d6d2b883bb4563e18fc2ce4d8 treats every bead carrying review labels as metadata-only by setting `recordClosingProvenance := !isMetadataOnlyReviewBead(beadBeforeClose)` and defining `isMetadataOnlyReviewBead` purely from labels. That is broader than the fix described in the commit message and FEAT-019: only metadata-only review closes should omit `closing_commit_sha`. Review beads can legitimately govern real docs or code changes, and this patch now drops their provenance even when the caller provides `--commit` or the close lands alongside real staged work. In a throwaway git repo with the current CLI binary, closing a normal bead with `--commit <short-head>` recorded `closing_commit_sha`, while closing an otherwise identical `action:review` bead with the same explicit commit omitted the field entirely. That makes replay and audit for non-metadata review passes fall back to sessionless/manual mode despite an exact implementation boundary being available.
Suggested fix: Narrow the suppression check to actual metadata-only review closes instead of all review-labeled beads, and add regression coverage for both explicit-commit and mixed-close review flows so non-metadata review beads still retain exact closing provenance.","id":"hx-03e23ec9","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:bead"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve closing provenance for non-metadata review beads","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:05:15.233027522Z"}

Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.

Finding filed: hx-2a146683.
Pass 1 correctness: the reviewed change fixes the original stale-provenance regression for in-repo review closes, and the added acceptance tests cover the intended preserve-valid-provenance and clear-tracker-only paths. A remaining correctness gap exists because commit inspection failures are treated as metadata-only tracker backfills, so a review bead with a valid pre-existing closing_commit_sha loses that provenance when closed in a non-git workspace.
Pass 2 integration: FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics require metadata-only suppression only when the command can prove the referenced SHA is a tracker-only backfill. The current helper instead erases provenance on any git-show failure, which breaks portable/manual review workflows outside a git repo.
Pass 3 concern-aware quality: implementation and tests stay aligned with go-std, testing, and security-owasp practices for the in-repo cases. No new attack surface or tooling drift was introduced in the reviewed scope.

Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit fb9bdf1 closes ddx-7e88c022 with closing_commit_sha=4563fa7, an abbreviated SHA. The close store documents this field as the exact closing commit SHA when known, but manual close evidence is persisted verbatim. Replay later resolves closing_commit_sha through git rev-parse to choose the baseline commit, so storing a short SHA weakens audit and replay determinism as the repository grows and abbreviations can become ambiguous or invalid. In the current tracker, this reviewed row is one of only two abbreviated closing_commit_sha values; the durable convention elsewhere is full 40-character SHAs.
Suggested fix: Normalize ddx bead close --commit input through git rev-parse before storing it, repair ddx-7e88c022 to the full 40-character 4563fa77d88cfb0f242925a927e913841b43868c value, and add regression coverage for manual close provenance normalization.","id":"hx-83ed9550","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: normalize closing_commit_sha to full git SHAs","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:54:27.646433594Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:2004:{"acceptance":"A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T10:50:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2270767","closing_commit_sha":"d51f18c93d88901d833c9cc82ab280611e7690d2","created_at":"2026-04-10T10:32:22.111979389Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:649
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.
Suggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","id":"hx-939516c7","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:51:40.697890368Z"}

Description: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. That is too broad: .ddx contains real governed artifacts such as config and other tracked workspace files, not just tracker backfills. In a temp repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, creating a bead, modifying a tracked .ddx/notes.txt file, and running ddx bead close lands a close commit that changes .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/notes.txt, but the bead still omits closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:567
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 1bc26181bae1e88cd6c29fc11f20c5904ed2ae8a normalizes `ddx bead close --commit` through `git rev-parse` before persisting the value. That fixes short SHAs inside a git repo, but it also makes manual close provenance unusable in non-git workspaces where bead commands otherwise still work. Reproduced with the built CLI in a temp directory outside git: `ddx bead create ...` succeeds, then `ddx bead close <id> --commit 1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678` now fails with `git rev-parse ... exit status 128`. Before this change, the command accepted an exact commit SHA verbatim. That is a behavior regression for portable/manual tracker workflows and contradicts the command contract that `--commit` records the exact closing commit SHA when known.
Suggested fix: Normalize only when the supplied value is an abbreviated ref in a git repository, but preserve already-full commit SHAs verbatim when git resolution is unavailable; add regression coverage for `bead close --commit <40-char-sha>` in a non-git workspace.","id":"hx-a77820e4","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve manual close provenance in non-git workspaces","updated_at":"2026-04-10T05:38:51.46880358Z"}

Description: During this review, `ddx bead close hx-01c0012d` closed a metadata-only review bead without creating a new git commit, yet the resulting tracker row recorded `closing_commit_sha=ff9a6ce2f6db77c670adcf2874b3f86278dba4e8`. That SHA is the previously reviewed hx-registry-lookpath provenance-repair commit, not the review-bead lifecycle change itself. Description: Commit 8eaf934b6d106b9f513fb76bb10f2091e67b87d5 improves ddx-83777abd by repointing its closing provenance at the actual implementation commit, but it stores only the 7-character prefix `d84aa2b` instead of the exact commit SHA. The tracker close contract documents `closing_commit_sha` as the exact closing commit SHA when known, and replay/audit use this field as durable provenance. The abbreviated prefix resolves today only because it is currently unique; as the repository grows it can become ambiguous or invalid, which would break later replay or audit runs for this bead.

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:403
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit c38d7bbb2cf47baed8197c27cac19cb4adcd8644 documents ddx bead update <id> --set closing_commit_sha=<sha> as the supported provenance-repair path and adds a happy-path test for closed beads, but the underlying bead update implementation still routes closing_commit_sha through the generic --set handler without any normalization or verification. Unlike bead close --commit, which resolves the value through git rev-parse --verify <sha>^{commit}, bead update accepts arbitrary strings such as not-a-sha and persists them to closed beads. That leaves FEAT-019 replay and audit provenance one bad repair away from silently storing an invalid or ambiguous commit boundary even though the docs now present this as the safe replacement for hand-editing storage.
Suggested fix: Teach ddx bead update --set closing_commit_sha=... to use the same commit-resolution path as bead close --commit and reject invalid or non-commit values, then add a regression test proving invalid repair SHAs fail and valid abbreviated SHAs are normalized to full commits.","id":"hx-fd99632b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:bead"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: validate closing_commit_sha repairs before persisting","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:02:53.028428892Z"}

storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions.
